L1.area: Storage layer query data access objects for the observability platform's JDBC-based backends

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the wildcard query functionality of the JDBC-based storage backends. User-supplied search keywords are directly concatenated into SQL query strings within LIKE clauses instead of being passed as parameterized query arguments, allowing an attacker to manipulate the executed SQL.

L3.area: H2 and MySQL JDBC storage query DAOs that implement wildcard search functionality
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the wildcard search functionality of the H2 and MySQL JDBC storage backends. When performing fuzzy queries for alarms, service metadata, endpoints, or traces, user-supplied search keywords are directly concatenated into SQL LIKE clauses instead of being passed as parameterized query arguments, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied search keywords are directly concatenated into SQL LIKE clauses in multiple JDBC query DAO classes, enabling SQL injection when using H2 or MySQL storage backends.
